items.md: with his money was complete, and, starting with "Only a Poor Old Man," it was understandable.

items.md: At the end of that story, after the Beagle  Boys have been routed,Donald leaves Scrooge standing at the edge of his huge pile, and says:  "You may not know it, Uncle Scrooge, but your billions are a pain in the neck. You're only a poor old man. Good-bye!" Scrooge stands staring blankly after him for a moment, then shucks his hat and cane as he prepares to plunge into his money. "Bah!" he says defiantly. "Kid talk! No man is poor who can do what he likes to do once in a while! And I like
